evidence: growth of semiconductor content in electronics has continued to drive demand for our products, particularly in the automotive, industrial and data center end markets
we invested $3.94 billion in R&D and SG&A, invested $4.55 billion in capital expenditures
Our Analog products are used in many markets, including industrial, automotive, data center, personal electronics and communications equipment

Breaches...could be caused by factors such as computer viruses, ransomware, malware, software vulnerabilities, system failures...use of artificial intelligence (AI) tools, or human error

capex_mentions.context: 2025 total capex; not disaggregated by AI/data center specifically

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: TXN is a semiconductor supplier to AI/data center markets, not a buyer of AI infrastructure; capex figures ($4.55B) relate to 300mm fab expansion broadly, not AI-specific spend; no AI-dedicated budget or AI infrastructure commitment disclosed

ai_narrative_concreteness: data center is named as a market sector (~9% of revenue) but no AI-specific product initiatives, deployments, or use cases are described; language is aspirational/market-positioning rather than operational

ai_risk_disclosure_depth: risk-section content is present; AI is mentioned specifically as a cybersecurity threat vector (use of AI tools by bad actors), and named governance roles (CISO, CIO) are described, qualifying as named company-specific risk categories with some governance detail, but no quantification or formal AI-risk program disclosed
